title: TRX Character Packing Codes (U, VU, and SU)
breadcrumbs: TRX Character Packing Codes (U, VU, and SU)
text: TRX Character Packing Codes (U, VU, and SU) The U, VU, and SU packing codes should be specified for any data that is to be stored in the Uniface transfer format (TRX). TRX has not been used since Uniface 8, so these packing codes are only available to ensure compatibility with older applications. Be sure to specify a length or use an asterisk (*). Uniface supplies an impractical default length if you do not specify one. Related Links Variable-Length Packing Codes Variable-length packing codes work with DBMSs that support a VARCHAR data type. They make it possible to store only the actual part of the field that is filled in, and not the trailing spaces that would be added for a fixed-length field. Segmented Fields: String Packing Codes (SC and SU) Segmented fields provide an alternative storage method for large amounts of data that would be impractical to store in a normal database column. Large data objects can include audio- and video clips, or objects that functionally belong to other applications but are under the control of Uniface. Such objects are referred to as Binary Large Objects (BLOBs). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
